code:
def longest_common_subsequence(s1, s2):
    m = len(s1)
    n = len(s2)

    # dp[i][j] will store the length of LCS of s1[0...i-1] and s2[0...j-1]
    # Initialize a (m+1) x (n+1) matrix with zeros
    dp = [[0] * (n + 1) for _ in range(m + 1)]

    # Fill the dp table in a bottom-up manner
    for i in range(1, m + 1):
        for j in range(1, n + 1):
            if s1[i - 1] == s2[j - 1]:
                dp[i][j] = 1 + dp[i - 1][j - 1]
            else:
                dp[i][j] = max(dp[i - 1][j], dp[i][j - 1])

    # Reconstruct the LCS string
    lcs_length = dp[m][n]
    
    # If LCS length is 0, return an empty string
    if lcs_length == 0:
        return ""
        
    lcs_chars = [""] * lcs_length  # Preallocate list for characters
    
    i = m
    j = n
    k = lcs_length - 1  # Index for lcs_chars, starting from the end

    while i > 0 and j > 0:
        if s1[i - 1] == s2[j - 1]:
            # If current characters are same, they are part of the LCS
            lcs_chars[k] = s1[i - 1]
            i -= 1
            j -= 1
            k -= 1
        elif dp[i - 1][j] > dp[i][j - 1]:
            # If not same, check which way led to the greater LCS length
            # If dp[i-1][j] is greater, it means s1[i-1] was not part of LCS
            i -= 1
        else:
            # If dp[i][j-1] is greater or equal, it means s2[j-1] was not part of LCS
            j -= 1
            
    return "".join(lcs_chars)
